div1.entryFree: Comprehend, to imagine: all this beforehand counsel --s, Lucr. 494. deeper sin than bottomless conceit can c. in still imagination, Lucr. 494 where all those pleasures live that art (== study) can c. Pilgr. 62 and LLL IV, 2, 114. shaping fantasies that apprehend more than cool reason ever --s, Mids. V, 6. if it would but apprehend some joy, it --s some bringer of that joy, Mids. V, 6 Blunderingly in Ado III, 3, 25 and III, 5, 50.
